column1: // VCBHN IAM Detection | Rule: Bulk Permission Changes
//   Detects when a single admin account makes an abnormally
//   high volume of permission or role assignment changes within
//   a short time window. In a healthcare IAM environment
//   bulk changes are rare and may indicate:
//     - Compromised admin account performing mass access grant
//     - Insider threat expanding access before exfiltration
//     - Unauthorized automation script running against directory
// NIST SP 800-66: AC-2 | AU-6 Audit Review
// Threshold:     5+ changes within 10 minutes by one initiator
// Response:      Immediately review all changes made. If no
//                and escalate to HIPAA Security Officer.
"Add app role assignment to service principal"
"Revert unauthorized changes and suspend account if no ticket found."
